text: Plumb must be installable via both pip and uv using the package name 'plumb-dev' on PyPI with CLI command 'plumb'.
Tests must support two formats for linking to requirements: comment-based markers using '# plumb:req-XXXXXXXX' format and function name-based linking using 'test_req_XXXXXXXX_' format.
The system must filter for spec-relevant content during decision extraction in both conversation analysis and diff analysis methods.
The system must implement semantic similarity checking to handle duplicate or similar decisions effectively.
Classification tasks must not generate reasoning traces to maintain optimal performance.
LLM-based deduplication must utilize Claude Haiku 4.5 as the designated model.
The deduplicate_decisions() function must accept a use_llm parameter that defaults to False to ensure backward compatibility while enabling optional LLM-based deduplication.
All interactions with LLMs must be implemented as DSPy programs, not open-ended agents.
Plumb must use the Anthropic Claude SDK as its inference provider with Claude Sonnet 4.6 as the default model for all programs.
Plumb must operate as a git hook and CLI tool without changing how users write code.
The pre-commit hook must ensure that every committed state has been reviewed and approved.
The system must prevent duplicate decisions through a multi-stage pipeline using exact matching, Jaccard similarity, and LLM semantic analysis.
Only prescriptive choices that affect system design, behavior, architecture, or data models must be captured as decisions.
Process observations, tooling choices, and diagnostic findings must be filtered out from decision capture.
Plumb must support loading environment variables from .env files to manage configuration settings.
The plumb init command must create a .env file in the project directory to facilitate environment-based configuration management.
The system must support ANTHROPIC_API_KEY configuration through both environment variables and .env files.
Tests must be linked to requirements through requirement ID comments to establish traceability between test functions and specific requirements.
All tests must be organized in the tests/ directory with specific test files for each component.
Tests without requirement links must be treated as sync violations that indicate either missing requirements or unnecessary tests.
The system must support file ignore patterns through a '.plumbignore' file.
The system must provide a '--all' command option for the 'plumb approve' command to approve all changes at once.
Generated cache and coverage files must be excluded from commits.
The system must include a 'check' command as an alias for manual decision scanning.
Plumb must handle conversations that span multiple Claude Code session files by reading and merging them chronologically.
The sync command must provide progress indicators with status updates to give users feedback during operation.
The workflow must require an explicit sync step after approving decisions, with staging of sync output before re-committing changes.
DSPy programs must support program-specific model configuration through a model configuration array in config.json.
Pattern parsing logic must be modularized into separate functions for reusability across programs.
Plumb must store all state in a '.plumb/' folder at the root of the user's repository that is committed to version control.
The system must track which requirements have been modified and only send dirty (changed) requirements to the mapper for processing.
The hook must validate API access before proceeding with analysis and exit non-zero if API authentication fails.
The hook must analyze the staged diff and Claude Code conversation log using the unified conversation reading system.
The system must auto-detect Claude Code sessions vs legacy logs and read from Claude Code's actual session files at ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-path>/<uuid>.jsonl.
The hook must write pending decisions to decisions.jsonl and set the last_extracted_at timestamp.
The hook must print a machine-readable JSON summary of pending decisions to stdout and exit non-zero, aborting the commit.
The system must include explicit safeguards to never approve, reject, or edit decisions on the user's behalf.
The commit must only land when there are zero pending decisions.
The pre-commit hook must validate API access before performing any LLM operations and block commits when authentication fails.
When API authentication fails, the system must raise a custom PlumbAuthError exception that alerts the user and provides clear instructions.
API key validation must be implemented as a separate validate_api_access function that is called before all LLM operations.
All authentication functionality must have comprehensive test coverage.
Existing tests must be updated to mock API validation calls to ensure test suites remain unaffected.
The system must support environment variable management using .env files with python-dotenv dependency.
The system must use gitignore-style patterns for ignore functionality, with default patterns used when no .plumbignore file exists.
After commit completion, the post-commit hook must clear the last_extracted_at timestamp to reset the filter for future extractions.
The conversation parser must handle Claude Code's type/message schema format and convert tool usage blocks to text format '[tool: Name] description'.
The system must include comprehensive documentation for the Plumb skill with detailed instructions for managing spec/test/code alignment and decision review processes.
The system must implement intelligent deduplication by first running Jaccard similarity filtering, then applying LLM deduplication as a second pass when 2 or more candidates remain.
The system must use DSPy's context manager with Haiku LM for deduplication while preserving Sonnet for other operations.
The system must generate complete and runnable tests rather than stub files with TODO comments.
Test generation must use increased context limits to accommodate complete test code.
The test generator must produce fully functional test code that can be executed immediately.
The hook must analyze the staged diff and read Claude Code's native session files directly from auto-detected paths.
The system must extract prescriptive choices while excluding observations and diagnostics.
Within-batch similarity deduplication must prevent duplicate decisions in the same batch.
Time awareness must use both last_commit datetime and last_extracted_at timestamp as cutoffs, taking the later of the two.
The system must use exact deduplication and LLM-based semantic deduplication with groq/openai/gpt-oss-120b configured for both decision_deduplicator and question_synthesizer.
Source summaries must be structured as per-file mappings to enable granular tracking.
All documentation files including SKILL files and CLAUDE.md must be kept consistent with the same sync workflow requirements.
The plumb init command must check that the current directory is a git repository and exit with an error if not.
The plumb init command must create the '.plumb/' directory if it does not exist.
The plumb init command must prompt the user interactively to provide paths to spec files and test files with validation.
The plumb init command must write '.plumb/config.json' with the provided paths.
The plumb init command must create a '.plumbignore' file in the project root if it does not exist.
The plumb init command must install the git pre-commit hook by writing a script to '.git/hooks/pre-commit' that calls 'plumb hook'.
The plumb init command must install the Claude Code skill locally by copying 'plumb/skill/SKILL.md' to '.claude/skills/plumb/SKILL.md' in the project root.
The plumb init command must append a Plumb status block to 'CLAUDE.md' at the project root.
The plumb init command must run 'plumb parse-spec' to do an initial parse of the spec into requirements.
The plumb init command must print a confirmation summary to the terminal, including confirmation that the skill was installed.
The plumb hook command must read '.plumb/config.json' and exit 0 silently if not found.
The plumb hook command must get the current staged diff via 'git diff --cached'.
The plumb hook command must detect amends by comparing the HEAD commit's parent SHA to last_commit.
The plumb hook command must detect broken references by checking all SHAs in decisions.jsonl against git history.
The plumb hook command must run the Diff Analysis DSPy program on the staged diff.
The plumb hook command must attempt to locate and read the Claude Code conversation log.
The plumb hook command must filter out decisions marked as non-spec-relevant during extraction.
The plumb hook command must merge and deduplicate decisions across chunks.
The plumb hook command must run Question Synthesizer for decisions with no associated question.
The plumb hook command must write all new decisions with status 'pending' to decisions.jsonl.
The plumb hook command must run 'plumb parse-spec' to update requirements cache for any modified spec files.
If pending decisions exist, the plumb hook command must check whether it is running in a TTY or as a subprocess.
In TTY mode, the plumb hook command must print a human-readable summary of pending decisions.
In non-TTY mode, the plumb hook command must print a machine-readable JSON object to stdout.
The plumb hook command must exit non-zero in both cases when pending decisions exist, aborting the commit.
If no pending decisions exist, the plumb hook command must run 'plumb coverage', print a brief summary, update config, and exit 0.
The hook must never exit non-zero due to an internal Plumb error.
The plumb diff command must preview what Plumb will capture from currently staged changes in read-only mode.
The plumb review command must provide interactive review of pending decisions for terminal use.
The plumb review command must read decisions.jsonl and filter for status 'pending'.
The plumb review command must accept an optional '--branch <name>' flag to filter by branch.
When multiple session files exist, the plumb review command must use the most recent session file by modification time.
The plumb review command must display decision details and prompt for user action.
After all decisions are resolved, the plumb review command must run 'plumb sync' for approved/edited decisions.
Decision classification must default the spec_relevant field to True when LLM classification is uncertain.
The plumb approve command must approve a single decision by ID and update its status to 'approved' in decisions.jsonl.
The plumb approve command must run 'plumb sync' for the approved decision only.
The status display must include a visual bar chart representation for coverage statistics.
The status display must use Rich's Status spinner that updates in-place for progress indication.
The plumb reject command must reject a single decision by ID and update its status to 'rejected' with the reason recorded.
The plumb edit command must replace the decision text for a given decision ID with user-provided text.
The plumb edit command must update status to 'edited' and run 'plumb sync' for that decision only.
The plumb modify command must modify staged code to satisfy a rejected decision.
The plumb modify command must read the decision object and verify status equals 'rejected'.
The plumb modify command must call the Claude API with staged diff, decision, rejection reason, and current spec.
The plumb modify command must apply the proposed modification to staged files.
The plumb modify command must run pytest to verify tests still pass after modification.
If tests pass, the plumb modify command must print the resulting diff and stage the modified files.
If tests fail, the plumb modify command must print failure output without staging the modification.
Plumb must never commit modifications, only stage them.
The plumb sync command must update the spec and tests to reflect all approved and edited decisions.
The plumb sync command must read decisions with status 'approved' or 'edited' that have not yet been synced.
For each decision, the plumb sync command must run Spec Updater to rewrite relevant spec sections.
Multiple decisions affecting the same spec section must be processed together in batches.
The plumb sync command must run Test Generator to generate organized tests for requirements not covered by existing tests.
Tests must be organized by functionality with proper requirement links in the format 'test_<req_id>_<description>'.
The test generator must read only source files referenced by file_refs on synced decisions for context.
The plumb sync command must write generated tests to 'test_generated.py' using temp file → rename.
The plumb sync command must run 'plumb parse-spec' to re-cache requirements.
The plumb sync command must set synced_at on each processed decision.
The plumb sync command must print a summary of spec sections updated and test files created.
The plumb sync command must use .plumbignore file with default patterns for documentation, build files, and IDE configurations.
The plumb parse-spec command must read all markdown files in spec_paths from config.json.
For each file or paragraph block, the plumb parse-spec command must run Requirement Parser to produce explicit, testable requirement statements.
The plumb parse-spec command must assign each requirement a stable ID based on a hash of its content.
The plumb parse-spec command must write results to '.plumb/requirements.json'.
Requirements with matching hashes must not be re-processed, preserving existing metadata.
The plumb parse-spec command must use regex patterns to extract requirement IDs from test files for coverage mapping.
The plumb parse-spec command must process all requirements and AST summaries through the CodeCoverageMapper in batch operations.
The plumb parse-spec command must implement granular per-file hashing with structured v2 cache format.
The plumb parse-spec command must extract source file paths from evidence strings using pattern matching.
The plumb coverage command must report coverage across code, spec-to-test, and spec-to-code dimensions.
For code coverage, the plumb coverage command must run 'pytest --cov' and parse output.
For spec-to-test coverage, the plumb coverage command must check whether tests reference or map to each requirement.
For spec-to-code coverage, the plumb coverage command must use the requirements cache to check implementation correspondence.
The plumb coverage command must print a formatted table using rich.
The plumb coverage command must use LLM calls to refresh the requirements cache for accurate coverage analysis.
The plumb coverage command must measure and report coverage improvement achieved by marker injection.
The plumb status command must print a human-readable summary including tracked spec files, number of tests, pending decisions, and coverage summary.
Plumb must ship with a Claude Code skill file at 'plumb/skill/SKILL.md'.
During plumb init, the skill file must be copied to '.claude/skills/plumb/SKILL.md' in the project root as a project-local installation only.
The skill file must provide structured guidance for AI-assisted development workflow.
The skill file must teach Claude Code the Plumb workflow and provide machine-readable protocol for parsing hook output.
The system must support reading Claude Code native session files with JSONL parsing from the ~/.claude/projects/ directory structure.
Conversation logs must be located in the real session JSONL files using the format ~/.claude/projects/<project>/<session-uuid>.jsonl.
The .claude/ directory and SKILL.md must be committed to version control so all contributors get the skill automatically.
plumb init must append a specific Plumb status block to CLAUDE.md at the project root, delimited by comment markers.
The Claude Code skill must never edit .plumb/decisions.jsonl directly.
The Claude Code skill must never edit .plumb/config.json directly.
The Claude Code skill must never install the Plumb skill globally.
The Claude Code skill must treat spec markdown files as the source of truth for intended behavior.
The Claude Code skill must not attempt to commit if there are decisions with status 'rejected_manual'.
When handling decision conflicts, the Claude Code skill must use opposite ordering rules: keep earlier decisions for duplicates and later decisions for countermands.
The conversation log reader must work seamlessly across different repositories without requiring additional user setup.
Plumb must attempt to auto-detect Claude Code log locations if not set in config.
If conversation log is not found, Plumb must skip conversation analysis and note 'conversation_available': false.
The conversation log must be a JSONL file with minimum fields: role, content, and timestamp.
Session files must be pre-filtered by modification time to avoid reading old files.
Plumb must read only turns recorded after the last_commit timestamp in config.json.
The conversation parser must handle the native session JSONL format with type/message schema used by Claude Code.
Chunking must be performed deterministically without LLM involvement using user turns as the primary unit.
If a chunk exceeds 6,000 tokens, it must be split at tool call boundaries or at the midpoint of the largest assistant turn.
Chunks must have one turn of overlap by prepending the final assistant turn of the previous chunk.
Before chunking, tool result turns longer than 500 tokens that appear to be file reads must be replaced with '[file read: <filename>]'.
Assistant messages that span multiple JSONL entries must be properly parsed and reassembled.
DecisionExtractor must be called once per chunk with identical diff_summary passed to every call.
Results must be merged after all chunks with near-duplicate decisions collapsed into one, preserving the earliest chunk_index.
On amends, the pre-commit hook must compare HEAD's parent SHA to last_commit and delete decisions where commit_sha equals last_commit before re-running analysis.
On every hook run, all stored SHAs must be checked against git history and unreachable SHAs flagged with 'ref_status': 'broken'.
Plumb must not attempt to re-map decisions to new SHAs after a rebase.
The decision log must be append-only with existing lines never modified in place.
Status updates must be written as new lines with the same id, with the latest line for a given id being canonical.
DiffAnalyzer must take raw unified diff string as input and output a list of change summaries with files_changed, summary, and change_type.
DiffAnalyzer must group related changes into logical units without inventing meaning.
DecisionExtractor must take a single conversation chunk and diff_summary as input and output a list of decision objects.
DecisionExtractor must extract explicit and implicit decisions but not trivial decisions like variable naming or import ordering.
QuestionSynthesizer must take a decision object with no associated question and output a plain-English question framing the decision.
RequirementParser must take markdown string as input and output a list of requirement objects with text and ambiguous fields.
RequirementParser must produce atomic statements in active voice with no duplicates and flag vague statements as ambiguous.
SpecUpdater must take spec_section and approved decision object as input and output updated markdown for that section.
SpecUpdater must capture the result of the decision as a natural requirement with no reference to the decision itself.
TestGenerator must take uncovered requirements, existing tests, and code context as input and output runnable pytest test code as a Python string.
TestGenerator must create one function per requirement with descriptive names and real assertions against actual code under test.
TestGenerator must not use pytest.skip() or TODO comments and must not overwrite existing tests.
CodeModifier must be implemented as a direct Claude API call, not a DSPy program, for open-ended code modification.
All CLI commands must fail gracefully with clear error messages if config.json is missing or malformed.
All DSPy programs must retry on LLM failure with maximum 2 retries then raise PlumbInferenceError.
The git hook must never exit non-zero due to internal Plumb errors and must print warnings to stderr while exiting 0.
File writes for spec updates and test generation must use temp file → rename to avoid partial writes.
If conversation log is unavailable, Plumb must continue with diff-only analysis without treating it as an error.
If plumb modify test run fails, Plumb must not stage the modification and must update decision status to 'rejected_manual'.
The plumb status command must operate in cache-only mode to avoid making LLM calls.
The plumb coverage command must display progress using an in-place updating spinner, then print the final results table.
Session file reading must be optimized by pre-filtering based on file modification times.
Plumb testing must use pytest with 80% coverage minimum for v0.1.0.
All CLI commands must be tested to run without error given valid inputs.
Per-decision commands must be tested to update decisions.jsonl correctly.
The coverage command must be tested to provide progress feedback with progress bar and status text.
Decision log functions must be tested for read/write/filter/dedup on .jsonl files with latest-line-wins logic.
Git hook must be tested to produce correct pending decisions given mock diffs and conversation logs.
Git hook must be tested for amend detection and TTY vs non-TTY output formats.
Conversation chunking must be tested for correct chunk boundaries, overlap, noise reduction, and metadata.
Each DSPy program must be tested to produce correctly structured output given fixture inputs for schema validity.
Coverage reporter must be tested for correct calculations given mock pytest output.
Sync functionality must be tested that spec and test files are updated correctly given approved decisions with no partial writes.
